title: Live integration tests against real org-mcp; reconcile tool-id and param drift
description:
The CLI was implemented mock-first. The mock + tests + src/contract.rs are internally consistent, but several tool ids and an unknown number of param names DO NOT match what the real org-mcp Emacs server registers. Until a live integration test runs, the CLI is silently wrong-shaped and would fail on first contact with a real server.

This ticket replaces three earlier tickets (org-cli-7zn live test scaffold, org-cli-w8n verify edit body params) and absorbs the integration-test work the user filed last. The deliverable is one suite that runs the existing tests/live_org_mcp.rs (gated on ORG_LIVE_TEST=1) against a real Emacs+org-mcp daemon AND a tool-id parity check that fails loudly if src/contract.rs drifts from ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el.

Confirmed drift (from grep of ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el ':id "..."' blocks vs src/contract.rs target fields):
  CLI registry            -> real org-mcp :id
  org-config-todo         -> org-get-todo-config
  org-config-tags         -> org-get-tag-config
  org-config-priority     -> org-get-priority-config
  org-config-files        -> org-get-allowed-files
  org-config-clock        -> org-get-clock-config
  org-edit-rename         -> org-rename-headline
  org-edit-properties     -> org-set-properties
  org-edit-tags           -> org-set-tags
  org-edit-priority       -> org-set-priority
  org-edit-scheduled      -> org-update-scheduled
  org-edit-deadline       -> org-update-deadline
  org-edit-log-note       -> org-add-logbook-note
  org-clock-status        -> org-clock-get-active
  org-clock-dangling      -> org-clock-find-dangling
  org-outline             -> org-read-outline

Confirmed matches (no rename needed):
  org-read, org-read-headline, org-update-todo-state, org-add-todo, org-edit-body, org-clock-in, org-clock-out, org-clock-add, org-clock-delete, org-ql-query, org-get-tag-candidates, query-inbox, query-next, query-backlog

Body param sanity check (from defun org-mcp--tool-edit-body in org-mcp.el):
  resource_uri, old_body, new_body, &optional append  --  matches what we shipped.

Param names for the 15 renamed tools above are NOT yet checked. Each renamed tool needs its defun signature inspected and any non-matching params reconciled.
design:
Runner: ~/.config/dotfiles/modules/home/emacs-gtd is a working home-manager module that already wires emacs30 + org-mcp + agile-gtd + the emacs-mcp-stdio.sh shim from mcp-server-lib. Use this as the integration runtime — do NOT build a parallel Emacs setup. The shim is installed at ~/.config/emacs/emacs-mcp-stdio.sh on the developer machine.

Source of truth for tool ids: ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el. Specifically each (mcp-server-lib-register-tool ... :id "X" ...) block from line ~2829 onward. Treat that file as the contract; src/contract.rs must mirror it.

Test layout:
  tests/live_org_mcp.rs already exists and is gated on ORG_LIVE_TEST=1. Extend it. Add per-tool happy-path round-trips for every registry entry, with an env-var ORG_LIVE_FILES=<path-to-test.org> for the read/write cases. Use a throwaway test file the test creates, mutates, and deletes.

Two new tests inside tests/contract_registry.rs (run by default cargo test, no ORG_LIVE_TEST gate):
  1. parity_with_org_mcp_el  -- parse ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el for ':id "..."' patterns, build a HashSet, assert every Tool-kind contract entry's target is in that set. The .el file path is computed from CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR (../org-mcp/org-mcp.el). If the file is missing (CI without sibling repo), the test logs a warning and passes — do not block builds when the sibling repo is absent.
  2. param_names_match  -- for each contract entry whose target matches a defun in the .el, parse the defun arglist and assert every CLI param's server_name is in the arglist. Same missing-file leniency.

Reconciliation procedure (do this in lockstep across mock + contract + main + tests):
  step A: rename target field in src/contract.rs entry to the real :id
  step B: rename mock dispatch arm in tests/fixtures/mock_org_mcp/main.rs
  step C: update src/main.rs handler if the param keys also drifted (only after reading the defun)
  step D: rerun cargo test; expect green

Order of work:
  1. Add the parity test red. It should fail on ~15 entries.
  2. Apply renames per the table in the description. After each rename: cargo test green, parity test green for that entry.
  3. With contract+mock aligned, run ORG_LIVE_TEST=1 cargo test --test live_org_mcp against the emacs-gtd daemon. Fix any param-name drift it surfaces.
  4. Once live + parity both green, document any remaining quirks (e.g. JSON shape diffs in responses) inline in src/contract.rs and as new bd tickets if they need user-facing changes.
acceptance_criteria:
1. tests/contract_registry.rs::parity_with_org_mcp_el passes against the current ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el — every Tool-kind contract.target is a registered :id in the .el. Test passes (with a warn log) when the .el is absent.
2. tests/contract_registry.rs::param_names_match passes — every ParamSpec.server_name is in the corresponding defun arglist. Same absent-file leniency.
3. ORG_LIVE_TEST=1 cargo test --test live_org_mcp -- --test-threads=1 passes end-to-end against an emacs-gtd-running daemon. Coverage:
   - handshake + tools/list + capability discovery (already shipped)
   - one happy-path tools/call round-trip per CLI command surface (read, read-headline, outline, query run, query inbox|next|backlog, todo state, todo add, all 8 edit subcommands, all 6 clock subcommands, all 6 config subcommands)
   - the read/write cases use a temp .org file passed via ORG_LIVE_FILES; the test creates it, runs operations, deletes it
4. src/contract.rs target field matches every :id in ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el for the 26 non-GTD core tools. The 15 renames listed in the description are applied in lockstep (contract + mock + main + tests).
5. Any param-name drift discovered during step 3 is reconciled and covered by an updated test.
6. README.md "See also" section already points at src/contract.rs and ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el — keep that, but add a one-line note that integration coverage is per this ticket.
notes:
Supersedes org-cli-w8n (verify edit body params; closed via supersede).
Supersedes the work intent in closed org-cli-7zn (the scaffold landed, but acceptance was insufficient).
The 15-rename table in the description was generated by:
  grep -E ':id "org-' ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el | sort -u
  vs
  grep server_name src/contract.rs entries with kind=Tool
A new agent picking this up should re-run that comparison first to detect any newer changes in the upstream repo.

The emacs-gtd module relies on a running services.emacs systemd --user unit. On a dev machine you can also start a one-shot emacs --daemon for the test session if the systemd unit isn't desired. The shim path is ~/.config/emacs/emacs-mcp-stdio.sh.

Live tests require an emacs daemon AND an .org file the user is OK being mutated. Use a temp file (not the user's actual GTD inbox) — the test must create + delete it.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 2: Command contract and schema layer
description: Introduce the command contract table and machine-readable schema discovery for the CLI surface. Define exact command-to-tool and command-to-resource mappings before broader command implementation.
design: Contract-first approach; codify parameter names, URI expectations, output schemas, and exit semantics as test-backed metadata
acceptance_criteria: org schema and org schema <command> return machine-readable metadata for the planned command surface; contract mapping tests pass
close_reason: Phase 2 complete: contract registry of 33 commands in src/contract.rs, lib target exposes registry, org schema and org schema <path> emit machine-readable metadata, edit body resource_uri quirk and clock in --resolve bool-as-string quirk encoded, 39/39 tests passing.

title: Phase 1: MCP client foundation
description: Create the Rust project skeleton and implement the synchronous MCP transport/client needed for initialize, tools/list, and tools/call. Include a fixture stdio server for protocol tests.
design: Hand-rolled sync client over stdio; explicit launcher command support; runtime framing assumptions verified by tests
acceptance_criteria: org tools list returns a valid CLI JSON envelope through the MCP client; handshake and protocol tests pass
close_reason: Phase 1 complete: sync MCP client, line-delimited JSON-RPC over stdio, mock org-mcp fixture (29 tools, MOCK_TOOL_ERROR + MOCK_NO_GTD knobs), CLI envelope, exit code matrix, 16/16 tests passing, clippy + fmt clean.

title: Finalize org CLI contract from rewritten plan
description: Use PLAN.md as the source of truth to lock the v1 CLI contract before implementation. Confirm the JSON envelope, launcher contract, schema command scope, GTD command discovery behavior, and strict per-command parameter mappings to org-mcp.
design: Agent-first CLI named org; JSON-first stdout; no TTY-driven format switching; preserve org-mcp semantics and verify transport assumptions live
acceptance_criteria: Accepted contract covers JSON envelope, launcher assumptions, schema/discovery scope, GTD discovery behavior, and command-to-tool/resource mapping rules
close_reason: Contract locked by PLAN.md as written; v1 envelope, launcher discovery rule, schema scope, GTD discovery, and per-command param mappings all stipulated. Ready to implement.

title: Live integration test EXECUTION against running emacs-gtd daemon
description: Acceptance #3 of org-cli-j8z requires live execution: ORG_LIVE_TEST=1 cargo test --test live_org_mcp -- --test-threads=1 passes end-to-end against an emacs-gtd-running daemon. The contract+param parity tests + scaffolded happy-path live tests are GREEN by themselves (gated on ORG_LIVE_TEST + ORG_LIVE_FILES). The remaining work is: (1) start emacs daemon (systemctl --user start emacs OR emacs --daemon for the test session); (2) prepare a throwaway test .org file and add it to org-mcp-allowed-files; (3) run the gated suite and reconcile any remaining drift the real server surfaces. This is a quick exercise once the daemon is up. Defer to live env.

title: Param drift reconciliation (org-set-properties: set/unset -> single 'properties' obj; rename* + scheduled/deadline/clock-in/out/delete time fields)
description:
Surfaced during org-cli-j8z parity work. Upstream defun signatures DIFFER from CLI server_name on:
- org-update-todo-state: 'from' -> 'current_state'
- org-add-todo: 'state' -> 'todo_state'
- org-rename-headline: 'from' -> 'current_title', 'to' -> 'new_title'
- org-set-properties: 'set'+'unset' -> single 'properties' object (CLI keeps --set/--unset surface; main.rs combines into properties object where v=null deletes)
- org-update-scheduled: 'date' -> 'scheduled'
- org-update-deadline: 'date' -> 'deadline'
- org-clock-in: 'at' -> 'start_time'
- org-clock-out: 'at' -> 'end_time'
- org-clock-delete: 'at' -> 'start'

Reconcile in: src/contract.rs (server_name), tests/fixtures/mock_org_mcp/main.rs (key reads), src/main.rs (key writes). Add param_names_match test in tests/contract_registry.rs that parses defun arglists from ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el. Will be done as part of org-cli-j8z.
close_reason: Closed

title: Phase 5: Todo command implementation
description: Implement org todo state and org todo add, including the required parameter shaping and URI normalization rules for org-mcp.
design: Respect org-mcp parameter expectations such as parent and after URI rules and preserve raw server error detail inside the CLI envelope
acceptance_criteria: Todo state update and creation commands are wired, contract-tested, and return stable JSON envelopes
close_reason: Phase 5 done: org todo state + org todo add wired with exact param mapping (uri/new_state/from/note for state; parent_uri/title/state/body/tags-as-array/after_uri for add). All optional flags omitted from arguments object when None; tags always sent as array. Contract registry corrected (server_name new_state, from). 85/85 tests pass.

title: Phase 4: Query command implementation
description: Implement org query and GTD query commands with explicit handling for optional server capabilities. The CLI must not assume GTD tools are always present.
design: Tool discovery drives optional GTD surface behavior; preserve org-mcp semantics and keep JSON output stable
acceptance_criteria: Core query path works; GTD capability handling is explicit and tested against discovered tool availability
close_reason: Phase 4 done: org query run + GTD query inbox/next/backlog wired; capability discovery via tools/list before GTD calls (returns kind=usage exit 2 if absent — exact PLAN §3.4 conditional behavior); mock returns realistic match payloads; 70/70 tests.

title: Upstream org-mcp: query pagination and projection
description: Propose and later implement pagination and projection support for org-ql-query so large result sets are manageable for agentic use and shell pipelines.
design: Agent-first scalability improvement for token budgets and large Org datasets
acceptance_criteria: org-ql-query supports bounded result retrieval and or field projection with backwards-compatible defaults
notes: DEFERRED: Cross-repo work — requires Emacs Lisp changes in ../org-mcp. Awaits explicit scope authorization to modify a different codebase.

title: Phase 9: Error handling and polish
description: Finalize exit-code behavior, preserve raw server error details inside the CLI envelope, add compact JSON mode, and write README examples for the accepted contract.
design: Do not add human-pretty rendering; polish focuses on machine-facing reliability and documentation
acceptance_criteria: Exit-code matrix is enforced, compact JSON mode works, and README examples match the implemented CLI contract
close_reason: Phase 9 done (and v1 complete): exit-code matrix systematically tested with new MOCK_DIE_AFTER_HANDSHAKE knob covering exit 3; --compact validated across all phase surfaces; server error.data preserved end-to-end (mock -> McpError::ToolError.data -> envelope error.data; bug fixed where it was always null); README written referencing PLAN.md as source of truth; readme_examples.rs keeps docs honest. 174/174 tests pass.

title: Upstream org-mcp: normalize tool URI inputs
description: Propose and later implement an upstream org-mcp change so tool inputs accept both bare and org:// URI forms. This removes a major class of CLI and agent mistakes.
design: Low-risk, high-value upstream change; keep resource behavior intact while relaxing tool input validation
acceptance_criteria: org-mcp tools accept both bare and org:// URI forms without changing existing semantics
notes: DEFERRED: Cross-repo work — requires Emacs Lisp changes in ../org-mcp. Awaits explicit scope authorization.

title: Phase 7: Clock command implementation
description: Implement clock status, in, out, add, delete, and dangling commands with careful handling of org-mcp clock semantics and odd parameter shapes.
design: Cover server-specific quirks such as clock resolve and value typing and keep output machine-stable
acceptance_criteria: Clock commands work through the CLI JSON envelope and pass contract and integration tests covering edge-case parameter shaping
close_reason: Phase 7 done: all 6 clock subcommands wired (status/in/out/add/delete/dangling); --resolve sent as JSON STRING per PLAN §5.6 §7 BoolAsString quirk, type-asserted in tests; clock out optional positional URI omits key when absent; mock has explicit echo handlers; 143/143 tests pass.

title: Phase 3: Read command implementation
description: Implement org read, org read-headline, and org outline using the contract layer and CLI JSON envelope. Ensure plain-text server results are wrapped consistently as JSON.
design: Special care for read-headline plain-text server output and outline file-path-oriented behavior
acceptance_criteria: All read commands return stable JSON envelopes; read command contract tests and integration tests pass
close_reason: Phase 3 done: src/uri.rs normalization (strip org:// before tool calls per PLAN §3.2), validate_outline_path rejects org:// (exit 2), org read/read-headline/outline wired with bare-form server calls verified via MOCK_RECORD_REQUESTS log, mock upgraded with realistic payloads, 57/57 tests, clippy+fmt clean.

title: Phase 6: Edit command implementation
description: Implement rename, body, properties, tags, priority, scheduled, deadline, and log-note edit subcommands with exact parameter mappings and stable JSON envelopes.
design: Per-command contract validation is mandatory, especially resource_uri versus uri and command-specific argument rules
acceptance_criteria: All edit commands map correctly to org-mcp parameters and pass contract and integration tests
close_reason: Phase 6 done: all 8 edit subcommands wired (rename/body/properties/tags/priority/scheduled/deadline/log-note); resource_uri quirk for body verified by dedicated test; --set k=v parsed into JSON object with first-= split; malformed --set gates exit 2 before server spawn; priority/date None sent as explicit null (clear semantics); 115/115 tests pass.

title: re-think the integration tests. don't start with systemctl. start with a regular startup script before the test run
description:
Replace ad-hoc / systemctl-driven emacs daemon orchestration in the live integration test path with a self-contained shell script that the test runner (Justfile or wrapper) invokes to (a) start a clean emacs daemon, (b) load org-mcp.el + emacs-mcp-stdio.sh, (c) export ORG_LIVE_TEST/ORG_LIVE_SERVER/ORG_LIVE_FILES, (d) run `cargo test --test live_org_mcp`, (e) tear the daemon back down. Goal: anyone can run the live tests without needing a long-running systemd user service.

Open questions:
  1. Where should the script live? tests/fixtures/live-emacs.sh? scripts/?
  2. Which emacs is canonical — system emacs, or the dotfiles emacs-gtd one at ~/.config/dotfiles/modules/home/emacs-gtd?
  3. Should the daemon use a fresh HOME / fixture .emacs.d so it doesn't taint the user's running emacs config?

title: in flake.nix don't install rust in the devshell. use fennix for building via CI or when building a package, but don't install the toolchain.
Cut GTD capability discovery from 2 RPCs to 1 (try-call + handle method-not-found)
description:
cmd_query_gtd in src/main.rs currently calls server_has_tool() (which round-trips tools/list) BEFORE calling the GTD tool. That is two RPCs where one would do for a fire-and-forget CLI.

Rewrite: call the GTD tool directly. If the server returns JSON-RPC error code -32601 (Method not found) or -32602 with a recognizable message, fall back to the same kind=usage envelope we emit today. Otherwise pass the response through.

Keep server_has_tool() in src/mcp/client.rs — other callers (tests, tools list) may want it. Just stop using it on the hot path.

Tests:
  tests/cli_query_no_gtd.rs already covers the MOCK_NO_GTD=1 path. Update its assertions: it should still produce the same envelope shape and exit code, but now via the call path, not the discovery path.
  Add an assertion that with MOCK_RECORD_REQUESTS=1 the request log shows exactly one request (the call) — proves we did NOT pre-call tools/list.
acceptance_criteria:
1. cmd_query_gtd path no longer calls tools/list. Verified by request-log assertion.
2. Behavior under MOCK_NO_GTD=1 is identical to today: kind=usage envelope, exit 2, message 'not advertised by server'.
3. Mock returns method-not-found error (code -32601) when MOCK_NO_GTD=1 and a GTD tool is called — adjust mock to do this if it currently silently echoes.
4. cargo test stays green end-to-end.
close_reason: Refactored to try-call+method-not-found; saves one RPC per GTD query. 5/5 cli_query_no_gtd tests green, 212 total tests passing. Mock now returns -32601 for GTD tools when MOCK_NO_GTD=1.

title: Transport timeout (--timeout flag, default 30s) on stdio recv()
description:
src/mcp/transport.rs::Transport::recv() does a blocking read with no timeout. If the launcher hangs after handshake, org hangs forever. The CLI advertises itself as fire-and-forget but currently has no upper bound on a single invocation.

Add a --timeout <seconds> top-level flag (default 30, env var ORG_TIMEOUT, 0 disables). Apply to every recv() call. On expiry: emit error envelope with kind=transport, exit code 3, message 'no response from server within Ns'.

Implementation:
  std::sync::mpsc + a reader thread, with recv_timeout() — keeps the rest of the code synchronous. OR: set a read timeout on the underlying File via libc fcntl/poll. The mpsc approach is simpler and matches the rest of the codebase.

Test:
  Add MOCK_HANG_MS=<n> knob to tests/fixtures/mock_org_mcp/main.rs that sleeps n ms before responding. Add tests/cli_timeout.rs verifying:
    - default succeeds when MOCK_HANG_MS is below threshold
    - --timeout 1 fails with exit 3 when MOCK_HANG_MS=2000
    - --timeout 0 disables the gate (test with a low MOCK_HANG_MS to keep the suite fast)
acceptance_criteria:
1. --timeout flag accepted at top level; ORG_TIMEOUT env var honored; 0 disables.
2. recv() honors the deadline across handshake AND per-call.
3. Hanging server triggers exit 3 with kind=transport envelope and a message naming the timeout.
4. New tests in tests/cli_timeout.rs cover the three cases above.
5. Default cargo test stays green; clippy + fmt clean.
notes: RED scaffolding done: MOCK_HANG_MS knob added to mock + tests/cli_timeout.rs covers default-succeeds, explicit-short-fails-with-exit-3-kind-transport, --timeout=0-disables, ORG_TIMEOUT env. 3/4 fail as expected (exit 2 since --timeout doesn't exist yet). GREEN step pending uml agent finishing main.rs/cli.rs PLAN cleanup.
close_reason: Closed

title: Add Justfile with dependency-update target (cargo + flake inputs + org-mcp pin reminder)
description:
The dotfiles emacs-gtd module pins org-mcp via nix-prefetch-github (~/.config/dotfiles/modules/home/emacs-gtd/update.sh). This CLI repo has no equivalent: cargo deps drift, flake.lock drifts, and there is no documented refresh path.

Add a Justfile at the repo root with at minimum:
  just update         # bumps cargo dependencies (cargo update), bumps flake inputs (nix flake update), and prints a reminder about how org-mcp itself gets refreshed in the dotfiles emacs-gtd module
  just update-cargo   # only cargo update
  just update-flake   # only nix flake update
  just check          # cargo test + cargo clippy + cargo fmt --check + nix flake check (mirror CI)
  just live           # ORG_LIVE_TEST=1 cargo test --test live_org_mcp -- --test-threads=1 (depends on org-cli-j8z; safe to commit before that ships, just runs the no-op gated tests)

The org-mcp Elisp source is fetched in dotfiles via emacs-gtd/update.sh; this repo only consumes ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el for the parity test (org-cli-j8z). Document that consumption clearly in the Justfile preamble.
design:
Use just (justfile) syntax. Keep targets thin — each target is one or two shell lines that delegate to the underlying tool. Do not reimplement cargo or nix logic in just.

Add just to the devShell in flake.nix so 'nix develop' provides it.

Do NOT pin org-mcp inside this repo's flake. The CLI is not a build dependency of org-mcp; it speaks to it over stdio. The parity test reads from ../org-mcp via a relative path; if the sibling repo is missing the test logs and passes (per org-cli-j8z).
acceptance_criteria:
1. Justfile exists at repo root with the targets listed in the description.
2. just check runs the same gates as .github/workflows/ci.yml.
3. just update is idempotent and safe to re-run; commits the lockfile changes is the developer's job, not just's.
4. README "Development" section references the Justfile.
5. nix-shell -p just --run 'just --list' prints all targets with one-line descriptions.
close_reason: Closed

title: push the nix flake to garnix via github CI. try to use a nix based build cache during CI build
close_reason: GitHub CI added: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs nix flake check + nix build via cachix/install-nix-action; configures cache.garnix.io substituter for cache hits from Garnix-built artifacts. garnix.yaml declares build matrix limited to x86_64-linux for checks/packages/devShells. README documents CI section + Nix install. 191/191 tests still passing locally.

title: build the package via github CI
close_reason: GitHub CI added: .github/workflows/ci.yml runs nix flake check + nix build via cachix/install-nix-action; configures cache.garnix.io substituter for cache hits from Garnix-built artifacts. garnix.yaml declares build matrix limited to x86_64-linux for checks/packages/devShells. README documents CI section + Nix install. 191/191 tests still passing locally.

title: create a flake.nix to build the package
close_reason: flake.nix added: nixpkgs-unstable + flake-utils. packages.default builds via rustPlatform.buildRustPackage; postInstall removes mock_org_mcp test fixture so only 'org' ships. devShell with cargo/rustc/rustfmt/clippy/rust-analyzer. apps.default + checks.default wired. Verified: nix build succeeds, all 190 tests pass in sandbox, ./result/bin/org --help works.

title: Verify edit body new_body/old_body and other edit param names against live org-mcp
description: Phase 6 chose new_body/old_body for org-edit-body and assumed snake_case for other edit tool params (set/unset/tags/priority/date/note). These are internally consistent but unverified against the real org-mcp Emacs server. Before v1 ship, run tests/live_org_mcp.rs (org-cli-7zn) and reconcile any drift. Update src/contract.rs and src/main.rs in lockstep.
notes: DEFERRED: Blocked on running ORG_LIVE_TEST=1 against real Emacs+org-mcp. The autonomous loop can't start the user's Emacs.

title: org query run vs bare org query <expr> surface mismatch
description: PLAN §6 specifies 'org query <ql-expr>' as a bare positional alongside 'org query inbox|next|backlog'. Phase 4 implemented 'org query run <ql-expr>' instead because clap-derive cannot cleanly express bare-positional + named-subcommand sibling. Close in Phase 9 polish: either keep 'run' as canonical and document, or use clap external_subcommand with manual flag parsing for bare form.
close_reason: Both done. qq7: discover_server() walks PATH, finds emacs-mcp-stdio.sh by is_file()+exec bit; missing -> exit 4 with PLAN §5.5 message. 9l0: clap-native path with args_conflicts_with_subcommands+subcommand_required=false; bare query <expr> dispatches as Run; named subcommands unchanged. 185/185 tests.

title: Live integration test against real org-mcp launcher
description: Add tests/live_org_mcp.rs gated on ORG_LIVE_TEST=1 that spawns the real emacs-mcp-stdio.sh and verifies framing, handshake, and at least one tool round-trip. Validates that the line-delimited framing assumption holds against the actual runtime.
close_reason: Live test scaffold landed in tests/live_org_mcp.rs gated on ORG_LIVE_TEST=1 (no-op pass when unset). 5 live tests: handshake, tools/list shape, outline (gated additionally on ORG_LIVE_FILES), capability discovery, envelope round-trip. README has Live integration section with env-var docs. 190/190 default tests pass.

title: Server auto-discovery in PATH for emacs-mcp-stdio.sh
description: When --server is omitted, search PATH for emacs-mcp-stdio.sh and use it. Otherwise fail with setup guidance pointing at PLAN §5.2. Currently --server is required and absent triggers a usage error.
close_reason: Both done. qq7: discover_server() walks PATH, finds emacs-mcp-stdio.sh by is_file()+exec bit; missing -> exit 4 with PLAN §5.5 message. 9l0: clap-native path with args_conflicts_with_subcommands+subcommand_required=false; bare query <expr> dispatches as Run; named subcommands unchanged. 185/185 tests.

title: Phase 8: Config command implementation
description: Implement read-only config and introspection commands for todo, tags, tag-candidates, priority, files, and clock and expose their schemas.
design: Configuration commands are low-risk validation and introspection primitives for agents and shell pipelines
acceptance_criteria: All config commands are available, schema-documented, and return stable JSON envelopes
close_reason: Phase 8 done: all 6 config subcommands (todo/tags/tag-candidates/priority/files/clock) wired with arguments:{}; mock has explicit response handlers; 152/152 tests pass.

title: Clean up PLAN.md references in code comments
description:
PLAN.md was deleted. Several inline comments and doc-strings still reference 'PLAN §X.Y':
  src/uri.rs:3, 22
  src/discovery.rs:3, 37
  src/cli.rs:121, 241, 334
  src/main.rs:222, 403, 504, 696
  src/contract.rs:4, 76, 524, 534, 839, 877

These references explain WHY a quirk exists, not what it does, so they are still load-bearing comments — just to a missing document. Replace each with either:
  a) a pointer to the equivalent location in ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el (e.g. resource_uri quirk -> 'see org-mcp--tool-edit-body defun')
  b) a self-contained explanation (one line) and removal of the dead pointer

Do NOT silently delete the comments — the WHY is the value. Just retarget them.

Run after org-cli-j8z so the org-mcp.el line numbers are stable (the integration ticket may shake things loose).
acceptance_criteria:
1. grep -rn 'PLAN \|PLAN.md' src/ tests/ returns zero matches.
2. Each former PLAN reference is replaced by either a defun/file pointer in ../org-mcp/org-mcp.el OR a one-line self-contained why.
3. cargo test, clippy, fmt all stay green (this is comment-only churn).
close_reason: Closed

title: --server-arg ergonomics: support '--' sentinel for trailing launcher args
description:
Today multi-arg launchers must be passed as '--server foo --server-arg --bar --server-arg --baz' which is awkward to type and noisy on the command line.

Add support for a '--' sentinel: 'org --server foo -- --bar --baz tools list' should put '--bar --baz' into the launcher argv. Keep --server-arg as a backward-compatible alternative.

clap supports trailing_var_arg + last(true) on a Vec<String> field, OR a manual argv split before clap parses. Manual split is more flexible because clap-derive's interaction with subcommands and --server is fiddly.

Update README usage examples to show the sentinel form as the recommended path.
acceptance_criteria:
1. 'org --server foo -- --extra=arg sub command' parses with launcher argv = [foo, --extra=arg], CLI subcommand 'sub command'.
2. Existing --server-arg form still works (no breaking change for current callers).
3. New tests in tests/cli_server_argv.rs cover both forms producing identical request logs against the mock.
4. README updated.
close_reason: Implemented + merged. 216 tests passing.

title: extract_result handles non-text MCP content variants (image, resource)
description:
src/main.rs::extract_result is a heuristic that looks at content[0].text and parses as JSON or wraps as {text:..}. It works for org-mcp by construction (the server only emits text content) but the MCP spec also defines image and resource content types. If the server ever returns one, the CLI silently throws it away.

Implement a typed walk over content[]: for each item, dispatch on type field (text/image/resource_link/embedded_resource/etc per MCP 2024-11 spec). For text: keep current parse-then-fallback behavior. For image: emit {type:image, mime_type, data} (base64 forwarded). For resource: emit {type:resource, uri, mime_type, text?}. If multiple items exist, emit data as an array preserving order; if exactly one item, keep current scalar shape.

Test:
  Add MOCK_RESPONSE_KIND=text|image|resource|mixed knob to tests/fixtures/mock_org_mcp/main.rs that swaps the content array shape for the next response. New tests/cli_content_variants.rs covers each.

Coordinate with org-cli-j8z. The integration test against real org-mcp may surface that the server already returns multi-item content arrays for some tool we did not anticipate.
acceptance_criteria:
1. extract_result handles text/image/resource variants explicitly, preserving the typed shape.
2. Multi-item content arrays produce data as an ordered array; single-item arrays keep the scalar shape so existing tests do not regress.
3. New tests/cli_content_variants.rs covers text/image/resource/mixed via the MOCK_RESPONSE_KIND knob.
4. cargo test stays green; existing tests/cli_read.rs etc. pass unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented + merged. 216 tests passing.

value: Phase 5 done. Convention: optional CLI flags are OMITTED from tool arguments object when None (don't send null). Vec params (e.g. tags) ALWAYS sent as array including empty []. URI-bearing fields go through normalize_for_tool. todo state -> org-update-todo-state with {uri,new_state,from?,note?}. todo add -> org-add-todo with {parent_uri,title,state,body?,tags,after_uri?}. Mock returns synthesized 'org://new-id-12345' for add. Server_name uses snake_case mirroring server tool param keys (new_state not new-state, after_uri not afterUri).

Phase 6 done. Edit body uses resource_uri (not uri) + new_body/old_body keys (executor's choice; verify against real org-mcp in live test). Edit properties --set k=v parses on first =, JSON object always sent (empty {}); --unset always array (empty []). Malformed --set returns kind=usage exit 2 before server spawn. Edit priority --priority absent -> explicit null (clear); same for scheduled/deadline --date. Edit tags zero --tag -> empty array (clear all tags).

Phase 1 done. Wire framing = newline-delimited JSON-RPC (line-delimited), confirmed working with mock. swap-point comment in src/mcp/transport.rs if Content-Length needed. mock_org_mcp binary at tests/fixtures/mock_org_mcp/main.rs spawned via env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_mock_org_mcp"). MOCK_TOOL_ERROR=<tool> -> -32000 error. MOCK_NO_GTD=1 -> omit GTD trio. Envelope: {ok:true,data:..} | {ok:false,error:{kind,code,message,data},exit_code}. Exit codes: 0 ok, 1 tool, 2 usage, 3 transport, 4 spawn. Modules: src/{main,cli,output}.rs + src/mcp/{mod,transport,client,error}.rs.

Phase 2 done. Contract registry at src/contract.rs holds CommandSpec/ParamSpec for all 33 PLAN §6+§6.1 commands. Lib target org_cli exposes contract+output. Quirks encoded in registry: edit body server_name=resource_uri; clock in --resolve ServerValue::BoolAsString; outline ParamType::FilePath; read-headline ServerReturns::PlainText. org schema [path] is internal (no transport). Phase 3 must strip org:// prefix before tool calls (PLAN §3.2 server rejects prefix in tool inputs even though CLI accepts EitherAccepted).

Phase 3 done. src/uri.rs has normalize_for_tool (strip org://) and validate_outline_path (reject org://, OutlineUriError::OrgUriNotAllowed -> kind=usage exit 2). Mock supports MOCK_RECORD_REQUESTS=1 + MOCK_REQUEST_LOG=<path>; tests assert bare-form was sent. Mock org-read/read-headline/outline return realistic payloads. Existing extract_result wraps PlainText as {text:...} automatically.

Phase 4 done. server_has_tool() in src/mcp/client.rs does tools/list scan (no caching). cmd_query_gtd checks discovery before sending — returns kind=usage exit 2 with msg 'not advertised'. cmd_query_run unconditional. Optional args (e.g. --tag) are OMITTED from tool arguments object when None (don't send null). Mock spawn inherits parent env so MOCK_NO_GTD=1 propagates by setting env on the test's Command. Surface deviation: implemented org query run "<expr>" instead of bare org query "<expr>" (clap-derive limitation) — tracked separately.

Phase 7 done. Clock in --resolve is sent as JSON STRING ("true"/"false"), NEVER bool — PLAN §5.6 §7 BoolAsString quirk. Tests assert serde_json::Value::String type explicitly. Clock out positional uri is optional; absent -> arguments.uri OMITTED entirely (no null). Status + dangling send arguments: {} (empty obj). Mock echo handlers preserve exact JSON value types so wire shape is testable.

Phase 8 done (org-cli-j8z). 16 tool :id renames + 10 param-name renames applied across src/contract.rs, src/main.rs, mock, and 7 test files. Renames: org-outline->org-read-outline, org-edit-* family->org-rename-headline/org-set-properties/org-set-tags/org-set-priority/org-update-scheduled/org-update-deadline/org-add-logbook-note, org-clock-status->org-clock-get-active, org-clock-dangling->org-clock-find-dangling, org-config-* family->org-get-{todo,tag,priority,clock}-config + org-get-tag-candidates + org-get-allowed-files. Param renames: from->current_state (todo state), state->todo_state (todo add), from->current_title + to->new_title (rename), set+unset MERGED into single 'properties' object (set k=v, unset k=null), date->scheduled, date->deadline, at->start_time (clock-in), at->end_time (clock-out), at->start (clock-delete). Contract registry parity tests (parity_with_org_mcp_el, param_names_match) green. 193 tests passing. edit-body still uses resource_uri+old_body+new_body+append (unchanged, was always correct).
